description: Biochemistry - enzymes, metabolism, molecular biology, signaling.

facts.context: Michaelis-Menten kinetics describes the relationship between:
distractors: enzyme tertiary structure and catalytic activity only
protein folding rates and temperature dependence
membrane fluidity and lipid composition

facts.context: Allosteric enzyme regulation involves effector binding at:
facts.truth: site distinct from the active site
distractors: the active site competing with substrate directly
the enzyme's N-terminus exclusively always
covalent modification sites on serine residues

facts.context: Competitive enzyme inhibition affects kinetic parameters by:
distractors: decreasing Vmax while Km remains constant
decreasing both Km and Vmax proportionally
increasing both Km and Vmax together

facts.context: Each turn of the citric acid cycle produces:
distractors: ATP directly without any electron carriers
only carbon dioxide as the sole product output
glucose regenerated from pyruvate molecules

facts.context: Oxidative phosphorylation couples electron transport to ATP synthesis via:
facts.truth: proton gradient across inner mitochondrial membrane
distractors: direct substrate-level phosphorylation reactions only
sodium-potassium pump driving ATP synthase
calcium ion flux through voltage-gated channels

facts.context: Net ATP yield from glycolysis per glucose is:
distractors: 4 ATP with no initial investment required
38 ATP including oxidative phosphorylation
0 ATP since glycolysis consumes all produced

facts.context: DNA polymerase requires for synthesis initiation:
distractors: no primer since it starts de novo synthesis
ATP hydrolysis to begin strand elongation
RNA template strand for all DNA synthesis
no modifications since mRNA is used directly
only poly-A tail addition without capping
intron retention as the default pathway
hyperbolic curve identical to myoglobin binding
linear relationship with oxygen partial pressure
inverse saturation decreasing with oxygen
direct ligand binding to DNA promoters
single-pass transmembrane ion channel opening
receptor tyrosine kinase autophosphorylation

facts.truth: phosphate transfer from ATP to substrates
distractors: removal of phosphate groups from proteins
peptide bond formation between amino acids
hydrolysis of ATP without substrate modification

facts.context: Molecular chaperones assist protein folding by:
distractors: directly catalyzing peptide bond formation
providing the final folded structure template
degrading all misfolded proteins immediately
